learning_outcomes:
1 1. Major
Programme: Upon completion of
the programme, students are able to: (1) provide
evidence-based care that supports physical and homeostatic functioning,
promotes psychosocial functioning and life-style changes, protects against
harm, recognizes the patients as a member of the family unit and community,
and leverages on effective use of the health-care delivery system (K/S/V). (2) apply
appropriate clinical and nursing therapeutic techniques unsupervised (K/S). (3) apply the
principles of health promotion in meeting the health needs of individuals,
groups, families and communities (K/S/V). (4) demonstrate
effective skills in communicating with patients, carers, peers and the health
care team (S). (5) coordinate
the care of clients in hospital and community settings (K/S). (6) apply
technology in the processing of information for nursing practice and research
(K/S). (7) provide
patient-centred care using the nursing process as a problem solving approach
(K/S). (8) recognize,
explain and manage health problems using basic, social, behavioural, clinical
sciences and managerial principles (K/S). (9) display
appropriate professional attitudes and apply relevant ethical and legal
principles in the practice of nursing (K/V). (10) demonstrate
competent decision-making, clinical reasoning and judgment skills to make
decisions and take actions (S/V). (11) demonstrate
understanding of self and engage in ongoing lifelong personal development
(V). (12) recognize
the roles of nurses within a health service institution and their influences
on its development, and engage in the development of nursing as a health-care
profession (K/V). (13) uphold the
professional code of conduct (K/V). (14) incorporate
cultural factors into the practice of nursing (V). K = Knowledge
outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and attitudes outcomes
study_scheme:
Nursing Nursing Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 112 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  10
MEDF1011, 1012, 1021, 1031 and 1041
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  99
NURS1151, 1162, 1163, 1164, 1242, 1403, 1603, 1701, 3121,
3126, 3127, 3131, 3135[a], 3136, 3142, 3143, 3144, 3145, 3148, 3152,
3153, 3154, 3157, 3159, 4102, 4121, 4122[a], 4123[a], 4124[a]
3.  |  Elective Courses:
1 course chosen from the following:  |  3
NURS2111, 2112, 2113, 2114, 2115, 2116, 2118, 2121,
2122
Total:  |  112
Explanatory Notes:
1. 2. [a]  |  NURS courses at 3000 and above level will be included
in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification. Students are allowed to take supplementary
examinations for all of the above courses. Capstone courses

Major Programme Requirement (for Associate Degree
or Higher Diploma holders admitted to senior-year places)
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 69 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  61
NURS3135[a], 3142, 3143, 3144, 3148, 3152, 3153, 3154,
3157, 3159, 4102, 4121, 4122[a], 4123[a], 4124[a]
2.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  5 units of courses chosen from the following: NURS3121, 3126, 3131, 3136, 3145  |  5
(b)  |  1 course chosen from the following: NURS2111, 2112, 2113, 2114, 2115, 2116, 2118, 2121, 2122  |  3
Total:  |  69
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  NURS courses at 3000 and above level will be included
in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students who lack the basic nursing training are
required to take foundation courses on a mandatory basis. The units of
foundation courses to be taken by each student may vary depending on the
previous disciplinary knowledge of the student concerned. Normally,
students taking foundation courses will not be able to complete all
requirements within the normative study period. Foundation courses include
MEDF1011, 1012, 1021, 1031, 1041, NURS1151, 1162, 1163, 1164, 1242, 1403,
1603, 1701, 3127 and 3315.
3. [a]  |  Students are allowed to take supplementary
examinations for all of the above courses including required, elective and
foundation courses. Capstone courses
